title: Contains Duplicate
description: Given an integer array nums, return true if any value appears at least twice in the array, and return false if every element is distinct.
thinking_questions: If you compared every pair, how many comparisons for n elements?
What data structure checks membership in O(1) average time?
What are you trading when you use a HashSet instead of nested loops?

hints.L1: Think about what you need to track as you scan — which data structure lets you check 'seen this before?' in O(1)?
hints.L2: Use a HashSet. For each element, check if it already exists — if yes, return true. If no, add it. One pass.
hints.L3:
// INSIGHT: trade O(n) space for O(1) lookup → O(n) instead of O(n²)
seen = set()
for num in nums:
    if num in seen:   # O(1) avg
        return True
    seen.add(num)
return False
# Alt: sort → check adjacent pairs → O(n log n) time, O(1) space

follow_up_questions: What if you needed to return the duplicate value itself?
With O(1) space required, what's your approach and complexity tradeoff?
When would you prefer sort+scan over HashSet?
solution_notes: HashSet one-pass: O(n) time, O(n) space. Sort alt: O(n log n), O(1) space.
